one_time_description: Correction of errors applied to opening accumulated operating surplus (Note 15)
economic_notes: Nation operates through multiple subsidiaries and joint ventures including construction, development, land development, resources, ventures, hospitality, and housing. Three joint ventures accounted for by proportionate consolidation: Seyem Qwantlen Construction/Milestone Environmental Contracting West (51%), Jackson Ridge Project (50%), Seyem Qwantlen/Matcon Civil Joint Ventures (51%). COVID-19 impacted cafe and gift shop operations due to Provincial Health Authority restrictions.
notes: Financial statements restated for prior year correction of errors of $234,470. Nation has complex consolidation with 16 fully consolidated subsidiaries and 3 joint ventures under proportionate consolidation plus 1 equity-accounted investment (25% in Weave Development Limited Partnership). Accumulated surplus split between accumulated operating surplus ($42,005,060) and accumulated remeasurement gains ($1,666,675). ISC funding total includes ISC base ($2,413,991) less ISC recovery ($2,442) plus ISC revenue deferred ($784,036) and FNHA ($557,417) less FNHA deferred ($46,958) and grants ($2,983,323) used for total_government_transfers calculation. osr calculated as total revenue minus ISC net, FNHA net, ISC deferred, grants and other government funding.
